S2: counts as elementally advantaged vs Electric, and every 120 normal attacks launches an attachable projectile (88.11% attach + 88.11% explosion, scaled by her passive attachment/explosion buffs). 2026-07-14 STRUCTURAL CORRECTIONS (MEASURED, three focus recordings + synthesis — see experiment-harness-ai.md 'RAPI SYNTHESIS FINAL'): (1) the 2808% burst nuke is a FLIGHTED missile landing ~0.4s post-banner INSIDE her window at the full buffed state (delaySec 0.4 — landing-time snapshot naturally grants FB +0.5, entry auras, flicker; recipe fits measured constants 27.9M/25.1M/32.0M at +0.02%/−0.4%/+1.1%) and is CHARGE-GATED (requiresPulls 120 — her fire-weak banner 1 at ~68 shots had no nuke; every >=120 banner did); (2) her burst's +421.2% attachment self-buff is MEASURED-INERT on every visible class (0.13% precision, 3 comps) and is REMOVED from the model (dead datamine entry 101631006 kept on record here; the same-branch S1 +95.04 IS live — the half-alive branch). But the engine zero-cores ALL her projectile (skill/burst) damage (DBG majors ∈ {1.0,1.175,1.5,1.675} = FB+crit only, never core), while her NORMAL bullets DO core. Adding a core term to her ~311M coreable projectile damage lifts ~830M→~1015M, ratio 0.72→~0.88 (≈60% of the deficit). This does NOT contradict the landed 'stickies never core' ruling -- that was the small out-of-burst ATTACH class (~340-620k), which genuinely doesn't core; the EXPLOSION class is a DIFFERENT hit type that does (Battery-6 datamine preamble already flagged 'sticky collisions ride the full bullet pipeline, core rate 200%'). Faithful fix = drop the fictional dot/storedHit X-placeholder, use TRUE explosion instance count WITH core.
